docs.name: Shader Mesh Gradient
docs.description: GPU-rendered mesh gradient: a fragment shader warps multiple OKLCH color blobs through 3-octave simplex noise so they flow continuously instead of locking into a static pattern. Pointer position subtly attracts the nearest blob; reduced-motion freezes a single curated frame; falls back to a static CSS radial gradient on devices without WebGL2.

props.name: colors
props.description: 3–6 color stops. Accepts hex, rgb()/rgba(), hsl(), or oklch(). Mixed in OKLab space inside the shader for perceptually uniform blends.

props.name: speed
props.description: Flow speed multiplier. uTime accumulates as deltaTime * speed.

props.name: pointerInfluence
props.description: How strongly the nearest blob is attracted to the cursor. 0 disables pointer reactivity.

props.name: grain
props.description: Film grain layered on top of the shader output. 0 disables. Keep below 0.1 to avoid banding.

props.name: className
props.description: Classes for sizing, rounding, and layout of the canvas wrapper.

props.name: children
props.description: Overlay content rendered crisply above the shader (text, CTAs, navbars).

docs.overview: ShaderMeshGradient renders a full-bleed animated mesh gradient on a single <canvas>. A WebGL2 fragment shader warps the sample point through 3-octave 2D simplex noise, then mixes the input color stops in OKLab space so the transitions stay perceptually smooth — no muddy mid-greys the way naïve sRGB blending produces. Each blob center drifts on a disjoint sine frequency, and the cursor exerts an exponentially-falling attraction on the nearest blob so the surface feels alive without becoming a draggable toy. On devices without WebGL2 the component degrades to a static CSS radial gradient using the same color stops; reduced-motion users see a single curated frame instead of the animation. Cap usage at ~2 instances per page — each runs its own RAF loop and GL context.

scenarios.title: Hero background with overlay copy
scenarios.description: Drop the gradient behind a hero headline. The canvas covers the parent, while children sit on top in a dedicated z-10 layer.

scenarios.title: Brand palette in OKLCH
scenarios.description: Pass any number of stops between 3 and 6. OKLCH gives you direct control over perceived lightness and chroma — so a muted teal won't visually outweigh a saturated coral.

scenarios.title: Calm card backdrop
scenarios.description: Drop pointerInfluence to 0 and slow speed for a non-interactive ambient surface — useful as the background of a marketing card or feature tile.
